title: Methodology for Verifying Eschatological Narratives
author: Nishaniyaan Research Board
summary: Explains why end-times predictions must adhere to strict Isnad validation and why fabricated (Mawdu) or weak (Da'if) narrations cannot be used to declare specific dates for future events.

sections.heading: Principles of Textual Authority
sections.content: The Qur'an is the supreme criterion. Hadiths must be authenticated by master muhaddithin (such as Bukhari, Muslim, Abu Dawud, Tirmidhi, and Al-Albani).

sections.heading: Distinction Between Prophecy & Observation
sections.content: A contemporary event (e.g. technological shifts) is an observation, not a dogmatic fulfillment unless supported by scholarly consensus.

title: Quarantined Internet Rumors & Fabricated Claims
author: Nishaniyaan Research Board
summary: Cataloging viral claims that lack authentic Hadith chains or scholarly support.

unverifiedItems.reasoning: Determining exact calendar dates contradicts explicit Prophetic statements that knowledge of the Hour's time belongs solely to Allah.
Graded severely weak or fabricated by Ibn al-Jawzi and Al-Aqili.
